item.title: An Emboldened New China Spells Trouble for Trump
item.description: We are entering a more perilous era where China no longer sees U.S. strength as something to fear.

item.title: Bill Cassidy Sold His Soul and Got Nothing in Return
item.description: A doctor and senator who voted to confirm Robert Kennedy still was snubbed by Trump in the Republican primary in Louisiana.

item.title: We’re at the Dawn of the Ozempic Era — and It’s Really Weird
item.description: One in eight American adults is taking GLP-1s. What does that mean for how we relate to our food, bodies and one another?

item.title: We’re at the Dawn of the Ozempic Era — and It’s Really Weird
item.description: One in eight American adults is taking GLP-1s. What does that mean for how we relate to our food, bodies and one another?

item.title: A Quietly Radical Experiment in Criminal Justice
item.description: Hawaii might have the answer.

item.title: Facing the Reality of Cellphones in School
item.description: Readers discuss the effects of cellphone bans in school and screen time limits. Also: Close “Alligator Alcatraz”; President Trump’s vulgar language.

item.title: We’re Thinking About Mental Health Diagnoses All Wrong
item.description: Neither genetics nor brain scans can distinguish a person with depression, A.D.H.D. or autism from one without.

item.title: The Horror of Sexual Assault in Israeli Prisons
item.description: Sexual violence by Israeli forces against Palestinians is widespread, according to new reports and harrowing first-hand accounts. The Opinion columnist Nicholas Kristof details a pattern of systemic abuse and “unrestrained power” within Israeli detention centers that challenges the moral silence of the international community.

item.title: A.I. Claims to Make Our Lives Easier. Does It?
item.description: The A.I. revolution involves a massive transfer of labor — not from worker to machine, but from worker to you.

item.title: The Silence That Meets the Rape of Palestinians
item.description: Male and female Palestinians describe brutal sexual abuse at the hands of Israel’s prison guards, soldiers, settlers and interrogators.

item.title: I’m a Democrat. My Party Has a Double Standard on Antisemitism.
item.description: When we’re talking about which allies to support or which communities we defend, our principles shouldn’t shift.

item.title: American Clothing No Longer Makes American Cultural Capital
item.description: Vintage American clothing still commands high prices, but the physical manifestations of U.S. empire have decayed. No one knows what’s next.

item.title: The Forests of the American West Are in Danger
item.description: The demise of pine forests could make snow virtually disappear from the Southwest in 50 or so years.

item.title: Cubans Deserve America’s Generosity, Not Its Cruelty
item.description: If Americans knew what Cuba was enduring, they would demand an end to the U.S. blockade.

item.title: The Platner Trap
item.description: The exact wrong answer to a Republican Party that’s flirting with fascism is a man who chose to put vile Nazi imagery on his own body.

item.title: The Atheist and the Machine God
item.description: What are the implications of artificial intelligence for religion?

item.title: For Some Patients, Cancer Is Becoming Like a Chronic Illness
item.description: For some patients, cancer is becoming like a chronic illness.

item.title: Hegseth Is Sending Us a Warning
item.description: The secretary of defense has argued that military retirees are subject to freedom of speech restrictions. Let that sink in.

item.title: Democrats Don’t Have to Campaign on Climate Change Anymore
item.description: Climate change is mainly a priority for voters already firmly in Democrats’ camp.

item.title: Democrats Who Are Soft on Republicans Have Got to Go
item.description: Virginians must fight back against its Supreme Court in the name of popular sovereignty.

item.title: How Students Interact With A.I. Is What Matters
item.description: Readers discuss artificial intelligence and writing in the classroom. Also: President Trump’s latest assault on science; election workers.

item.title: A 3-Year-Old Is Looking for Answers
item.description: “Why are some things special?” This Mother’s Day, we revisit a 3-year-old’s endless curiosity and a mother’s joy of seeing the world through her child’s eyes.

item.title: Molly Jong-Fast on Her Mother, Erica Jong, on Mother’s Day
item.description: I’m realizing that a sort of gooey sitcom resolution is not in the cards for my mother and me.

item.title: The N.B.A. Lottery Is Broken
item.description: How the N.B.A. can fix its tanking problem.

item.title: Why China Is So Much Less Scared of A.I. Than the U.S.
item.description: Different strokes for different folks.

item.title: This Is What Happened After We Gutted U.S.A.I.D.
item.description: Trump’s most lethal policy will almost surely be his 71 percent cut in humanitarian aid from 2024 to 2025.

item.title: GLP-1s and the ‘Wild West’ of Wellness
item.description: GLP-1s are already a blockbuster drug. But does their popularity stem from how they work, or the strange culture into which they’ve landed? On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Ezra Klein talks to the health journalist Julia Belluz about the strange science and cultural impact of these blockbuster drugs.

item.title: How GLP-1s Suppress Our Hunger. (It’s Weird.)
item.description: GLP-1s enable weight loss in a very weird way. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” Ezra Klein talks to the health journalist Julia Belluz about the strange science and cultural impact of these blockbuster drugs.

item.title: My Ted Talk
item.description: Captain Courageous cut a cool swath, in contrast to today’s creepy oligarchs.

item.title: High Gas Prices Are Just the Beginning
item.description: How the economic shock waves of Trump’s Iran war will be felt globally.

item.title: Our Elections Are Broken
item.description: Black Americans in the South will likely lose most of their federal representation as a result of the Supreme Court’s recent erosion of the Voting Rights Act, the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues. This is only one of the reasons the United States needs to transform the current Electoral College system.

item.title: The Abortion Pill Case Is a Disaster for Republicans
item.description: Making abortion illegal doesn’t stop abortions; it just stops safe ones. As the Supreme Court weighs a huge restriction on the abortion drug mifepristone, Molly Jong-Fast explains the three options the Supreme Court might take.

item.title: A Dairy Farm’s Last Days Expose a Broken System
item.description: Readers respond to Eli Saslow’s front-page report about the Watson family’s farm in Pennsylvania.

item.title: High Gas Prices Are Just the Beginning
item.description: How the economic shock waves of Trump’s Iran war will be felt globally.

item.title: Graham Platner Is No Nazi
item.description: Conservative attacks are premised on a deliberate misreading of both the candidate and his progressive fans.

item.title: Live: Ezra Klein Moderates California Housing Forum With Candidates for Governor
item.description: The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein moderates a forum with five of the top candidates for governor of California.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Is Running Meta Into the Ground
item.description: Meta has commenced a long, slow slide into irrelevance.

item.title: Banning Phones in Schools Is Still a Good Idea, Despite Disappointing Data
item.description: School phone bans aren’t magic. We still need them.

item.title: The Unintended Symbolism of Trump’s ‘Triumphal’ Arch
item.description: What Trump’s monuments look like to a British architecture writer.

item.title: The Iran War Will Cost Trillions
item.description: The war in Iran will cost trillions — far more than current estimates.

item.title: There Is a Better Way to Get Kids Off Screens
item.description: Our research shows what works. It’s not a complete ban.

item.title: What to Do for the Elderly? Stop Badgering Them!
item.description: Readers respond to an essay about old people preparing (or not) for the end. Also: Antisemitism online; Trump the destroyer; two types of dreaming.

item.title: America’s Arrested Development
item.description: The American economy and the climate would both benefit from reform of permitting rules.

item.title: Here’s How Freaked Out You Should Be About the Hantavirus Cruise Ship
item.description: An expert weighs in.

item.title: The Voting Rights Act Changed America. Now, It Can Change.
item.description: Do we care when other groups can’t elect their favorite candidates?

item.title: True Believers Blow Trump’s Mind
item.description: Our most transactional president is having trouble processing.

item.title: Is Trump About to Invite In the Biggest Predator in the World?
item.description: Chinese automakers do not belong on American soil.

item.title: A Legendary Investor on How to Prevent America’s Coming ‘Heart Attack’
item.description: Why Ray Dalio is bearish on the U.S.

item.title: Is America Headed for ‘Greater Disorder’?
item.description: “I think we know that we are in increasingly disorderly times,” the billionaire investor Ray Dalio says on “Interesting Times,” where he tells the columnist Ross Douthat why he’s pessimistic about America’s future.

item.title: Talk to Strangers, and Share Your Worlds
item.description: Why talking to strangers is good for you. Also: Ballroom math; Haitian refugees; pro-Republican avatars; the Potomac challenge; 1976 and 2026.
